rationale: In the theory section, the citing paper uses Chen, Ng, and Rao (2005) to support a core premise that Eastern versus Western cultural orientations differ in impatience and in present-versus-future focus. That premise is then used to motivate why high versus low power distance belief should map onto restraint and impulsive buying. The citation is not method reuse, but it plays a meaningful conceptual framing role rather than a purely passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Chen, Ng, and Rao (2005) as prior empirical evidence on cross-cultural impatience/time orientation, and uses it to ground its argument that culture-linked beliefs can influence self-control-related consumption. This helps set up the citing paper's hypotheses about power distance belief reducing impulsive buying.
